mentions.note: Ross asked for diversification; stock still needs Chinese buyers; had nice bounce on cyclical buying; be a trimmer here, not buyer.
Chemical play working alongside Lyondell; polyolefins exposure; Cramer endorses as winning pair.
Polyethylene producer up 78% Q1 on Iran-war petrochemical shortage; bounced in January before war on rate-cut hopes; risk pullback if strait reopens but could persist. Cramer says 'ka-ching.'
Up 78% Q1 on petrochemical shortage; benefits from rate-cut expectations and Iranian supply disruption; at risk if strait reopens.
Commodity chemical beneficiary of petrochemical shortage from war disruptions. Destroyed capacity provides durable supply support; Cramer believer in owning.

Up 65% YTD; Strait of Hormuz closure driving record petrochemical margins (10¢/lb March, 20–30¢ April, more May); logistics disruption extends through 2026–27. Q2 guidance $12B revenue, $2B EBITDA, trajectory to ~$6B year.
Cited as a cyclical commodity chemical maker whose earnings are hostage to the economic cycle; used as an illustrative example in a fundamentals episode, not a current recommendation.
